freeSearchText: ID: 1412  Path: MAG 0334 "L'obbligo di vincere", in "Autosprint", year XII, no. 15, April 10-171972 (1412)  obbligo di vincere in Autosprint year XII no. 15 April 10-171972 obbligo di vincere (tr. The duty to win) in Autosprint year XII no. 15 April 10-17 1972 pp. 26-28. The article featured in Autosprint magazine details series of test sessions held at Ferrari private Fiorano circuit with strong focus on Enzo Ferrari philosophy regarding racing technology and driver development. The Fiorano track constructed in 1972 adjacent to the Maranello factory is presented as crucial laboratory for continuous technical improvement and driver training. Unlike conventional race circuit its design incorporates various types of corners found on Formula calendars of the time emphasizing rigorous testing and data collection rather than public spectacle. Enzo Ferrari himself closely monitors these tests which aim to identify develop drivers capable deeply understanding the car providing precise feedback. articulates that obligation win obbligo di vincere is moral imperative for him as victory validates technical choices made repays trust placed by collaborators fans. Losing therefore is considered failure. He expresses criticism modern Formula increasing focus on television advertising lamenting its move away from being pure test man machine.  His approach favours engineers who can translate driver sensations into technical solutions drivers who develop profound symbiotic relationship with their car akin heat conductors transmitting vital information. article also touches upon continuous pursuit progress stating that real work begins Monday after race as new discoveries from each event drive further development. His authority direct involvement Scuderia ethos are evident throughout narrative reinforcing his role ultimate decision-maker driving force behind relentless quest for excellence.< accompanying photographs offer glimpse Enzo hospitality newly built Fiorano track.  April 10-17 1972 10-04-1972 17-04-1972 Rivista settore Mario Morselli Marcello Sabbatini Autosprint MAG 0334 MAG 0334 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Magazines 932 935 1315 MAG 0334 year XII no 15 10 17 1410 no 10 171972 1412

detailsTitle: "L'obbligo di vincere", in "Autosprint", year XII, no. 15, April 10-171972
detailsDescription: L'obbligo di vincere (tr. The duty to win) in "Autosprint", year XII, no. 15, April 10-17, 1972, pp. 26-28. The article, featured in Autosprint magazine, details a series of test sessions held at Ferrari's private Fiorano circuit, with a strong focus on Enzo Ferrari's philosophy regarding racing, technology, and driver development.    The Fiorano track, constructed in 1972 adjacent to the Maranello factory, is presented as a crucial "laboratory" for continuous technical improvement and driver training. Unlike a conventional race circuit, its design incorporates various types of corners found on Formula 1 calendars of the time, emphasizing rigorous testing and data collection rather than public spectacle. Enzo Ferrari himself closely monitors these tests, which aim to identify and develop drivers capable of deeply understanding the car and providing precise feedback.    Ferrari articulates that "the obligation to win" ("l'obbligo di vincere") is a moral imperative for him, as victory validates the technical choices made and repays the trust placed by collaborators and fans. Losing, therefore, is considered a failure. He expresses criticism of modern Formula 1's increasing focus on television and advertising, lamenting its move away from being a pure "test of man and machine." His approach favours engineers who can translate a driver's sensations into technical solutions and drivers who develop a profound, symbiotic relationship with their car, akin to "heat conductors" transmitting vital information.    The article also touches upon Ferrari's continuous pursuit of progress, stating that the "real work" begins "the Monday after the race," as new discoveries from each event drive further development. His authority and direct involvement in the Scuderia's ethos are evident throughout the narrative, reinforcing his role as the ultimate decision-maker and driving force behind Ferrari's relentless quest for excellence.<    The accompanying photographs offer a glimpse of Enzo Ferrari's hospitality on the newly built Fiorano track.

freeSearchText: ID: 1461  Path: MAG 0467 "Non comincia da Parigi la fine dell'auto", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 41, October 7-14, 1975 (1461)  Non comincia da Parigi la fine dell auto in Autosprint year XV no. 41  October 7-14 1975 Giancarlo Cevenini Non comincia da Parigi la fine dell auto (tr. The end of the car does not begin in Paris) in Autosprint year XV no. 41 October 7-14 1975 pp. 40-41. Pininfarina delivered an exceptional design with the 308 GTB (which purposely does not bear the Dino name as it may be retired from Maranello production) crafting car that successfully combines elegance assertiveness prestige and power. Its low sleek profile represents highest and most current expression of two-seater Gran Turismo. The car offers acceleration and stability worthy of Ferrari name notable active passive safety features relatively competitive fuel consumption pricing (when compared to cars its class). Its debut at Paris Motor Show was an immediate success. The 308 GTB features body made entirely fiberglass (excluding hood) by an outside firm (Bagni) Reggio Emilia that also supplies Maranello F1 prototype bodies. It is powered by same central-mounted 8-cylinder engine found Dino-Bertone with displacement 2926 cc delivering 255 HP at 7200 rpm maximum speed 252 KMH. The aerodynamic design was refined Pininfarina wind tunnel resulting controlled downforce for better road adherence without negatively impacting drag coefficient (CX). interior is modern comfortable with elegant complete instrumentation particular attention was paid to driving position. Available options include air conditioning power windows stereophonic radio addition to leather interior metallic paint. price this Ferrari including VAT is 14 950 000 lire. version engine size slightly below two liters planned for future which would allow buyers evade higher 30% VAT rate applied engines over 2000 cc (as opposed 12% rate for smaller engines). Deliveries Ferrari 308 GTB are scheduled begin at end November across Europe pending final homologation export USA by end year. detailsTitle: "Non comincia da Parigi la fine dell'auto", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 41, October 7-14, 1975
detailsDescription: Giancarlo Cevenini, Non comincia da Parigi la fine dell'auto (tr. The end of the car does not begin in Paris), in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 41, October 7-14, 1975, pp. 40-41.     Pininfarina delivered an exceptional design with the 308 GTB (which purposely does not bear the "Dino" name, as it may be retired from Maranello production), crafting a car that successfully combines elegance, assertiveness, prestige, and power.  Its low, sleek profile represents the highest and most current expression of a two-seater Gran Turismo. The car offers acceleration and stability worthy of the Ferrari name, notable active and passive safety features, and relatively competitive fuel consumption and pricing (when compared to cars in its class). Its debut at the Paris Motor Show was an immediate success.  The 308 GTB features a body made entirely of fiberglass (excluding the hood) by an outside firm (Bagni) in Reggio Emilia that also supplies Maranello's F1 and prototype bodies. It is powered by the same central-mounted 8-cylinder V engine found in the Dino-Bertone, with a displacement of 2926 cc, delivering 255 HP at 7200 rpm, and a maximum speed of 252 KMH.  The car's aerodynamic design was refined in the Pininfarina wind tunnel, resulting in controlled downforce for better road adherence without negatively impacting the drag coefficient (CX). The interior is modern and comfortable, with elegant and complete instrumentation, and particular attention was paid to the driving position. Available options include air conditioning, power windows, and a stereophonic radio, in addition to leather interior and metallic paint.  The price of this Ferrari, including VAT, is 14,950,000 lire. A version with an engine size slightly below two liters is planned for the future, which would allow buyers to "evade" the higher 30% VAT rate applied to engines over 2000 cc (as opposed to the 12% rate for smaller engines).  Deliveries of the Ferrari 308 GTB are scheduled to begin at the end of November across Europe, pending final homologation for export to the USA by the end of the year.

preferredLabel: "Ferrari risponde a tutti (e su tutto)", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 43, October 21-28, 1975
freeSearchText: ID: 1462  Path: MAG 0469 "Ferrari risponde a tutti (e su tutto)", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 43, October 21-28, 1975 (1462)  Ferrari risponde tutti (e su tutto) in Autosprint year XV no. 43  October 21-28 1975 Ferrari risponde tutti (e su tutto) in Autosprint year XV no. 43 October 21-28 1975 pp. 7-10. Enzo Ferrari responds to several questions sent in by Autosprint readers. Below is one of the questions asked to Enzo to answer he takes inspiration from the new recently launched on the market LA NUOVA FERRARI 308 GTB MARCO NONNIS di Tortoli Nuoro Lei pensa che il recente titolo conquistato F. porti un beneficio alla vendita delle sue vetture di serie pensa che oggi il cliente faccia la sua scelta senza lasciarsi trascinare dalla pubblicità che arreca un titolo di campione del mondo? ENZO FERRARI La pubblicità emerge da un titolo mondiale ha influenza sulle vendite anche se forma indiretta. giusta la sua seconda ipotesi il cliente esige una bella vettura comoda confortevole sicura scattante. Apprezza la bella meccanica le prestazioni alto livello caratteristiche una vera granturismo razza derivata dalle corse. La nostra recente 308 GTB stata concepita con attenzione queste esigenze incontrerà penso molti consensi anche indipendentemente dal campione del mondo aggiunge comunque prestigio all immagine. English translation MARCO NONNIS Tortoli Nuoro Do you believe that recent title won F1 benefits sale of your production cars or do you think that today customer makes their choice without being swayed by publicity that world championship title brings? ENZO FERRARI  The publicity emerges from world title does influence sales albeit indirectly. Your second hypothesis is correct customer demands beautiful convenient comfortable safe and responsive car. They appreciate fine mechanics and high-level performance characteristics of true racing-derived grand touring car. Our recent 308 GTB was designed with these demands mind and will believe meet with wide approval even independently world championship which nevertheless adds prestige image. October 21-28 1975 21-10-1975 28-10-1975 Rivista settore MAG 0469 Italiano Cartaceo MAG 0469 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Magazines 932 935 1439 MAG 0469 year XV no 43  October 21 28 1454 risponde tutti su tutto no  October 21 28 1462

detailsTitle: "Ferrari risponde a tutti (e su tutto)", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 43, October 21-28, 1975
detailsDescription: "Ferrari risponde a tutti (e su tutto)", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 43, October 21-28, 1975, pp. 7-10.  Enzo Ferrari responds to several questions sent in by "Autosprint" readers.     Below is one of the questions asked to Enzo Ferrari; to answer, he takes inspiration from the new Ferrari recently launched on the market:    LA NUOVA FERRARI 308 GTB  MARCO NONNIS di Tortoli, Nuoro: Lei pensa che il recente titolo conquistato in F. 1 porti un beneficio alla vendita delle sue vetture di serie o pensa che oggi il cliente faccia la sua scelta senza lasciarsi trascinare dalla pubblicità che arreca un titolo di campione del mondo?  ENZO FERRARI: La pubblicità che emerge da un titolo mondiale ha influenza sulle vendite, anche se in forma indiretta. E' giusta la sua seconda ipotesi: il cliente esige una bella vettura, comoda, confortevole, sicura, scattante. Apprezza la bella meccanica e le prestazioni di alto livello, caratteristiche di una vera granturismo di razza derivata dalle corse. La nostra recente 308 GTB è stata concepita con attenzione a queste esigenze e incontrerà, penso, molti consensi anche indipendentemente dal titolo di campione del mondo, che aggiunge comunque prestigio all'immagine.     English translation:  MARCO NONNIS di Tortoli, Nuoro: Do you believe that the recent title won in F1 benefits the sale of your production cars, or do you think that today's customer makes their choice without being swayed by the publicity that a world championship title brings?   ENZO FERRARI: The publicity that emerges from a world title does influence sales, albeit indirectly. Your second hypothesis is correct: the customer demands a beautiful, convenient, comfortable, safe, and responsive car. They appreciate the fine mechanics and high-level performance, characteristics of a true racing-derived grand touring car. Our recent 308 GTB was designed with these demands in mind and will, I believe, meet with wide approval even independently of the world championship title, which nevertheless adds prestige to the image.

freeSearchText: ID: 1466  Path: MAG 0466  "La Ferrari parigina", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 40, September 30 - October 7, 1975 (1466)  La Ferrari parigina in Autosprint year XV no. 40  September 30 October 1975  La Ferrari parigina (tr. The Parisian Ferrari)  in Autosprint year XV no. 40 September 30 October 1975. The Ferrari 308 GTB debuts at the Paris Motor Show. The two-seater car designed by Pininfarina is presented as the successor to the Dino 308 GT4 by Bertone. car debut was previously announced at post-Monza press conference. These are some technical characteristics of new Ferrari. Transverse rear engine 81 71 2926 cc 225 HP at 7000 rpm. speeds limited-slip differential ventilated discs with brake booster 252 km/h 7250 rpm standing kilometer in 25 4. car has something of BB in nose and tail central part bears stylistic mark of Pininfarina. chassis remains tubular while wheelbase drops to 2340 mm compared to 2550 Dino-Bertone. Weight 1090 versus 1150 kilograms September 30 October 1975 30-09-1975 07-10-1975 Rivista di settore Autosprint MAG 0466 Italiano Cartaceo MAG 0466 Historical Archive 42 Photographic inventory 926 Magazines 932 935 1975 1439 MAG 0466 year XV no 40  September 1464 La parigina no  September 1466

detailsTitle: "La Ferrari parigina", in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 40, September 30 - October 7, 1975
detailsDescription: La Ferrari parigina (tr. The Parisian Ferrari), in "Autosprint", year XV, no. 40, September 30 - October 7, 1975.     The Ferrari 308 GTB debuts at the Paris Motor Show. The two-seater car, designed by Pininfarina, is presented as the successor to the Dino 308 GT4 by Bertone. The car's debut was previously announced at Ferrari's post-Monza press conference.  "These are some technical characteristics of the new Ferrari. Transverse rear 8 V engine, 81 x 71, 2926 cc, 225 HP at 7000 rpm. 5 speeds, limited-slip differential, ventilated discs with brake booster, 252 km/h at 7250 rpm, standing kilometer in 25"4. The car has something of the BB in the nose and in the tail; the central part bears the stylistic mark of Pininfarina. The chassis remains tubular, while the wheelbase drops to 2340 mm, compared to the 2550 of the Dino-Bertone. Weight 1090 versus 1150 kilograms".
